_note: DRY content families for SA signs that are NOT bare codes in the official chart (sa_relevant was false). One Brave-grounded template per family, expanded per variant by scripts/signs/apply-external-drafts.mjs. Tokens: {v} {who} {vehicle} {condition} {restriction} {code}. Each family must cite a primary official source (SARTSM on transport.gov.za / NATIS).

braveQuery: South Africa road sign R201 minimum speed limit end of minimum speed K53 meaning
SARTSM regulatory signs R201 speed limit sign maximum speed South Africa learner licence meaning rule
snippetExcerpts: The R201 speed limit sign is a regulatory traffic sign that indicates the maximum speed allowed on a particular road.
an R Series regulatory sign used to indicate the maximum legal speed that may not be exceeded from the point of installation

template.plainEnglish: You may not drive faster than {v} km/h from this sign onwards. {v} km/h is the maximum allowed — not a speed you have to reach.
template.formalMeaning: Regulatory sign R201: the maximum speed limit on this section of road is {v} km/h. It applies from the sign until another speed-limit sign cancels or changes it.
template.behaviour: Be at or below {v} km/h by the time you reach the sign, and stay at or below {v} km/h until another speed sign changes the limit. Slow down further when conditions (rain, traffic, pedestrians) make {v} km/h unsafe.
template.commonMistake: Treating {v} km/h as the speed you must drive. It is the highest legal speed only — you may not exceed {v} km/h to keep up with other traffic, and you should go slower when it is not safe.
template.testHint: A red ring around a black number means MAXIMUM speed. Here the number is {v}, so {v} km/h is your ceiling.
